verbGlossary.ACCEPTED BY: {source}formally accepts a completed punch item resolution as satisfactory for closeout.
verbGlossary.APPROVED BY: Senior {target} grants formal authorization for an action plan or corrective strategy to proceed.
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED BY: {source} delegates a punch item to the responsible trade or crew member for correction.
verbGlossary.AUTHORED BY: {source} creates the primary written content of an incident record or action plan.
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZED BY: {source} classifies a punch item by issue type during walk-through or review.
verbGlossary.CLOSED OUT BY: {source} completes all remaining punch item work and formally marks the resolution as finished.
verbGlossary.COORDINATED BY: {source} aligns schedules, disciplines, or third-party visits to ensure inspections proceed without conflict.
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: Standard {target} staffing measure has a statistical relationship with a quality or safety outcome metric.
verbGlossary.DISPOSITIONED BY: Design {target} determines the accept, reject, or repair disposition of a non-conformance report.
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED BY: {source} creates the formal written record of a deficiency or inspection item finding.
verbGlossary.ESCALATED TO: Senior {target} is formally notified when a safety violation exceeds severity thresholds.
verbGlossary.EVALUATED BY: {source} reviews individual inspection checklist items and assigns pass, fail, or deficient status.
verbGlossary.EXECUTED BY: {source} physically carries out the corrective action or action plan task in the field.
verbGlossary.FILED BY: {source} archives the record into the project document management system for audit and compliance.
verbGlossary.FOLLOWED UP BY: {source} conducts re-inspection or verification that a deficiency notice has been properly addressed.
verbGlossary.IDENTIFIED BY: {source} discovers and formally documents a coordination issue, design conflict, or constructability problem.
verbGlossary.INITIATED BY: {source} creates a new observation or finding record in the quality/safety system based on field conditions.
verbGlossary.INVESTIGATED BY: {source} conducts a formal root-cause inquiry into an incident, interviewing witnesses and reviewing evidence.
verbGlossary.ISSUED BY: {source} formally releases a violation, NCR, deficiency notice, or corrective action to the responsible party.
verbGlossary.LOGGED BY: {source} enters the record into the project tracking system and assigns a control number for workflow.
verbGlossary.MANAGED BY: {source} oversees the full lifecycle of a quality or safety record from creation through closeout.
verbGlossary.NOTIFIED OF: Senior {target} is formally informed of a significant incident requiring executive awareness.
verbGlossary.PERFORMED BY: {source} physically executes an inspection, system test, or commissioning activity on site.
verbGlossary.PROVIDED BY: Field {target} furnishes a witness statement or firsthand account for an incident record.
verbGlossary.REPORTED BY: {source} creates and submits the initial incident or safety event report into the formal system.
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {source} examines a record for accuracy, completeness, and compliance before it advances in workflow.
verbGlossary.SCHEDULED BY: {source} sets the date, scope, and responsible parties for a planned inspection or quality activity.
verbGlossary.SIGNED OFF BY: Owner or authority {target} provides final approval on a punch item, clearing it for acceptance.
verbGlossary.WITNESSED BY: {source} is present during a test, inspection, or material pour to verify procedures and results.
